warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: The Fukui-reactivity calculations for pyridine were performed using DFT at the level of theory B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF), with a gas-phase environment (no solvent). The molecular structure was taken as-is from the provided pyridine.xyz file without further relaxation. The simulation assumed charge 0 and singlet multiplicity 1. The partial charge scheme used was Mulliken, and the calculated Fukui plus reactivity index (f+) at the nitrogen atom (N, atom-index 0-based: 0 / atom-label 1-based: 1) was found to be precisely 0.48422572. The calculation integrity was deemed trustworthy, although warnings received include (1) sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge), (2) sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge), and (3) common header present. The provided results can be reproduced following the reported method specifications and input file path: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz.
